CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-03-2018 Continued To Oct-03-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-20-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jodie Steinberg, Esq. 408-755-9793 of Hann Law Firm for Plaintiff Seung Ku Kang.
Dean Paik, Esq. 415-956-2828 of Roger Joseph O'Donnell for Defendant SUNG HO CHO, YOUNG JIN BAE.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares it's views. Defendant Argues. The Court rules on evidentiary objections 1 through 6. The evidentiary objections are sustained in part and Overruled in part. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court also looks at the Christian Research case cited by Defendant. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SUNG HO CHO, YOUNG JIN BAE'S MOTION TO STRIKE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 425.16.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Sung Ho Cho and Young Jin Bae's anti-SLAPP motion to strike the sole cause of action for defamation in the complaint filed by plaintiff Seung Ku Kang is denied. Mr. Kang's asserted second cause of action for punitive damages is not a separate claim from his first cause of action for defamation for purposes of this motion. Mr. Cho and Mr. Bae satisfied their first prong burden of showing that the advertisement in the Korea Times was made in a public forum in connection with an issue of public interest (CCP 425.16 (e)(3).) Weinberg v. Feisel (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1122 is distinguishable. There, the defendant believed that the plaintiff stole his token and ran an advertisement in a newsletter regarding the plaintiff in an attempt to oust plaintiff from the token association. Weinberg involved a private matter-one person stealing a token from another. This case involves a public matter-one person allegedly stealing funds from a community organization that affects numerous persons. Mr. Kang satisfied his second prong burden of showing that his defamation claim has minimal merit. The evidence on this motion shows that Mr. Kang can prove a prima facie case of libel. Defendants' objection to paragraph 7 of Mr. Kang's declaration as lacking foundation is sustained. The declarations of Mr. Cho and Mr. Bae, however, show that they are members of the board of the Korean American Community Center and they took a responsible part in the publication. Mr. Kang's declaration, which denies the charges, presents sufficient evidence of malice to require that the malice issue be resolved by the trier of fact. Even if Mr. Kang is a limited public figure, which is disputed because he did not voluntarily thrust himself into a public issue, his declaration is sufficient to show a triable issue regarding actual malice. Mr. Kang's fee request is denied because the motion was not frivolous.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
